mnemonics: ARIMA FORECAST: Always Remember, It's Mostly About Forecasting Over Recent Changes And Smoothing Trends
keyPoints: Calculate the forecast (ŷ) using the fitted ARIMA model equation.
Compute the standard error of the forecast (SE_fcst) using the formula involving the AR and MA coefficients and the error variance.
Determine the probability limits (confidence intervals) using ŷ ± (z-value * SE_fcst), where the z-value corresponds to the desired confidence level.
commonMistakes: Using the wrong z-value (e.g., using a t-value or misidentifying the confidence level) for the probability limits.
Forgetting to account for all the terms (AR lags, MA lags, error terms) when calculating the forecast standard error, leading to an incorrect interval width.
Confusing the forecast standard error with the standard error of the model residuals, which are used in its calculation but are not the same thing.
